name: Kishka - Easier
recipeIngredient: 3 slices White bread (tear into small pieces)
1 tablespoon Minced onions (mix in hot water) or 1 small onion grated
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, melt margarine with hot water until completely melted.
2. Gradually add flour, bread, corn flakes, and minced onions to the melted mixture.
3. Stir in freshly ground pepper and paprika until well combined for a reddish color.
4. Line two baking sheets with foil, measuring 15 inches long each.
5. Shape the mixture into two long rolls on the foil, about 2 inches from each edge.
6. Each roll should be approximately 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
7. Bake at 350°F for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
8. Allow to cool slightly before wrapping in fresh tin foil and freezing.
9. When ready for use, slice the kishka and place on a cookie sheet to warm thoroughly.
10. Makes about 12 pieces. Optionally, serve with mushroom sauce or beef sauce.
